rule: All projects managed by codex-developer must have a package.json and a vercel.json file in the root directory before any deployment attempt.

VERCEL.JSON SCOPING: Before generating vercel.json, scan package.json and imports. Only include env references for services actually used (Clerk, Stripe, Supabase, Resend, PostHog, Sentry). If none are used, skip vercel.json entirely and let Vercel auto-detect the framework.

SUPABASE PACKAGE: For Next.js web projects, use '@supabase/supabase-js' and '@supabase/ssr' — NEVER the bare 'supabase' package (it's a CLI tool with native binaries that fail on Android ARM64 and aren't needed for web apps).
NEVER rewrite a working file from scratch. Always READ the current file first. Make surgical changes. If a feature already exists, skip it.
Before generating tasks, list all existing files and their key functions. Generate tasks only for MISSING features.
Maximum 40 lines changed per cycle. Use git diff to verify.
Always verify syntax before commit. On failure, revert and log.
Python imports: use module-relative imports (from models import X), NOT package-absolute (from backend.models import X) unless PYTHONPATH is set.
Python imports: use module-relative imports, not package-absolute, unless PYTHONPATH is set.
IMPORTS: Use correct import paths. If tools.py has web_search(), agent.py imports: from tools import web_search.
IMPORTS: Use correct import paths. main.py imports from agent.py and tools.py, does not redefine.
NO INVENTED IMPORTS: Only import from files that exist in the project or are standard libraries. If you need a component, create it first. Never import from paths that do not exist.

IMPORT RESOLUTION: Before using any import from @/components or ./ relative paths, verify the file exists. If importing from a file that does not exist, either CREATE that file first or REMOVE the import. Never leave broken imports.
NAMING: Match function/class names EXACTLY as specified. web_search not tool_web_search. Agent not run_agent.
NAMING: Match function/class names EXACTLY as specified.
NO DUPLICATION: Do not copy function definitions into multiple files. Each function lives in ONE file. Import it elsewhere.
NO DUPLICATION: Each function lives in ONE file. Import it elsewhere. Never redefine.
COMPLETENESS: Every function must have a real implementation. No pass, no TODO, no placeholder comments.
NO PLACEHOLDERS: Every function must have a real, working implementation. No pass, TODO, or placeholder text.
COMPLETE CONTENT: Every section you add must have real content. If adding Features, include actual feature descriptions. If adding a Hero, include real headline and subtext. No empty placeholders.
SELF-PROTECTION: Never modify files in the codex-developer skill directory.
SELF-PROTECTION: Never modify files in \$SKILLDIR.
NO MARKDOWN FENCES: Never wrap code output in ``` fences. Output raw code only. The first line of your response must be the actual code, not a markdown fence.
INCREMENTAL PATCHING: When modifying an existing file, read the FULL file first. Only change the requested sections. Keep ALL existing code intact. Do not remove working features.
WORKING LINKS: Every navigation link must point to an existing page or section. If the target does not exist, create it. No dead links.
ERROR RESILIENCE: Wrap dynamic imports and auth checks in try/catch. If a service is unavailable, show a graceful fallback message instead of crashing the page.

ERROR BOUNDARIES: For React/Next.js pages, wrap the main component in an error boundary. Use try/catch in the default export. Show a graceful fallback message instead of crashing the page on import errors.

FOR WEB PROJECTS: Use Next.js 16 App Router with TypeScript. Use Tailwind CSS and Shadcn UI for styling. Use Supabase for database/auth. Use Clerk for authentication. Use Recharts for charts. Use pnpm as package manager.
STACK DETECTION: If the project has package.json, it IS a web project and MUST use the VibeStack. If no package.json, determine the appropriate stack from the request.
ANDROID PLATFORM: When building Next.js projects on Android/Termux (aarch64), use Next.js 16.x which has WASM fallback. Version 14.x SWC binaries are not available for Android.
NO UNNECESSARY DEPENDENCIES: Do not import from packages not in package.json. For theme providers, use a simple pass-through wrapper. Never add imports that require npm install.
NEXT.JS CLIENT COMPONENTS: Every file using React hooks MUST start with "use client" as the first line. Server components cannot use hooks. Without this directive, the build fails.
TSCONFIG AUTO-GENERATION: For Next.js projects, always create tsconfig.json with paths: {@/*: [./*]}. This enables @/components imports.
CLERK PROVIDER REQUIRED: Any page or component using Clerk hooks MUST have a parent layout.tsx with <ClerkProvider> wrapping children.
CLERK PROXY: For Next.js 16+ with Clerk, proxy.ts MUST export default clerkMiddleware from @clerk/nextjs/server. NEVER create custom proxy handlers.
CLERK NEXT.JS 16 INCOMPATIBILITY: Clerk v5 does NOT work with Next.js 16. Use guest mode as fallback. Or use NextAuth.js instead of Clerk.
NATIVE MODULE BLOCKLIST: Never include these npm packages: canvas, sharp, puppeteer, playwright, node-gyp, bcrypt, sqlite3. These require native compilation and fail on Android.
FILE EXTRACTION FILTER: Never extract bare version numbers, framework names, or garbage tokens as filenames. Only extract strings that look like actual file paths with extensions.
PACKAGE.JSON GENERATION: For any Node.js/Next.js project, ALWAYS create package.json with proper dependencies before building other files.
DEPENDENCY PRECISION: Only include packages that are actually imported in the project. Never add native-compilation packages for browser functionality.

PRE-FLIGHT AUDIT: Before proposing any code modification or import structure, perform a mandatory audit of the actual filesystem (e.g., using 'ls -R '). All proposed imports must map to existing files discovered in this audit. If a module is missing, create it first before importing it. Strictly enforce this to prevent ModuleNotFoundError/hallucinations.

CROSS-FILE INTERFACE VALIDATION: Before generating a file that imports from another project module, READ the target module first. Verify that every imported class/function name actually exists. Never import 'SessionManager' if the module only has 'SessionHandler'.

IMPORT PATH CONSISTENCY: When main.py imports from local modules, use flat imports for flat projects and package imports for package projects. Never mix 'from ui import' with 'from src.ui import' in the same project.

CONFIG KEY CONSISTENCY: When one file writes config keys (gateway_ip) and another reads them, use identical key names. Never use 'router_url' in one place and 'gateway_ip' in another for the same value.

CLASS vs FUNCTION IMPORTS: Check whether the target module uses classes or functions. If ui.py has 'class RouterUI', import it as a class and instantiate it. If it has 'def display_header', import the function directly. Never mix the two patterns.

PROJECT COMPLEXITY DETECTION: Before generating phases, count the actual files needed. If ≤5 files, use FLAT structure — all files at project root. No src/, no lib/, no __init__.py. If 6-15 files, use simple folders (src/). If 16+, use full package structure.

POST-BUILD STRUCTURE AUDIT: After all files are built, check if Python files are nested 3+ levels deep in a simple project. If so, flatten to root and fix imports automatically. Empty directories must be removed.
NO UNNECESSARY FILES: Do not generate __init__.py, pyproject.toml, or setup.py unless the project explicitly requires packaging. Simple CLI tools and scripts need only the .py files requested.
TWO-ROUND RESEARCH: After initial research, perform gap analysis — ask 'What critical details are missing?' Then do a second research round to fill those gaps. Never build on incomplete research.

ENVIRONMENT-SPECIFIC RESEARCH: When the project targets a specific platform (Termux, Android, router, API), ask about real endpoints, auth mechanisms, and hardware constraints. Do not assume a REST API exists unless confirmed.
HEALER STOP CONDITION: If the healer runs 3 times on the same file without success, mark the file as permanently failed and move on. Never infinite-loop on an unfixable file.

QUEUE vs DONE SYNC: Before starting the build loop, if QUEUEFILE and DONEFILE have the same number of entries, clear DONEFILE. A full queue with all entries marked done means the loop will exit immediately.
STALE LOCK CLEANUP: At the start of main(), remove any lockfile older than 10 minutes. A stale lock from a killed process should never block a new build.
ABSOLUTE PATH DETECTION: In apply_file(), if the filepath already contains the full REPODIR path, strip REPODIR before joining. Never create paths like /home/user/project/home/user/project/file.py.
TILDE EXPANSION IN FILENAMES: Never use ~ in generated file paths. Always expand to absolute paths or use project-relative paths. The queue must never contain entries like NEW:~/router-ctrl/main.py.

STRENGTHEN OUTPUT SANITIZATION: After the strengthen LLM call, strip any markdown formatting, bullet points, or metadata that the LLM appended outside the FILE: block. Only the code between FILE: and end-of-file should be saved.

IMPORT CROSS-REFERENCE IN STRENGTHEN: During the strengthen pass, scan the file's imports against other project modules. If any imported name doesn't exist in the target module, flag it in the strengthen prompt so the LLM can fix it.
GITHUB DEFAULT BRANCH: Use 'main' as the default branch for new repos. Set git config --global init.defaultBranch main to suppress the 'master' warning.
GITIGNORE MINIMUM: Every project must have .gitignore containing at minimum: .codex/, reviews/, CHANGELOG.md, __pycache__/, *.pyc, .env, *.log
STACK PLUGIN SILENCE: Stack plugins (vibestack, flaskstack, vanillastack) must only log when they ACTIVELY assist. When stepping aside, be completely silent. No 'Not a VibeStack project' noise.

PREFER SIMPLICITY: When multiple approaches exist, choose the simplest. Flat files over nested packages. One file over three. Direct imports over complex module paths. The user can always refactor later.

ERROR VISIBILITY: Never suppress stderr with 2>/dev/null on critical operations. If recon, hermes, or build steps fail, the user must see the error. Only suppress expected warnings, not actual failures.

NO PACKAGING FOR SIMPLE PROJECTS: If the project has ≤5 files total, do NOT generate pyproject.toml, setup.py, setup.cfg, or __init__.py. These are only needed for installable packages. Simple CLI tools and scripts run directly with python3.

CODEX DNA SIGNATURE: Every generated file MUST include exactly ONE of these patterns. (1) Python: a comment line reading '# ctx: codexhaven' immediately after the last import. (2) JavaScript: a comment line reading '// ctx: codexhaven' immediately after the last import. (3) Shell: a comment line reading '# ctx: codexhaven' after the shebang. (4) Config files (JSON/YAML/TOML): a key or comment named '_ctx' with value 'codexhaven'. This is NOT optional. This is the Codex Developer signature.

PYTHON STRING QUOTING: ALL string values in Python code MUST be quoted. Never write 'tokenUrl=token' — always 'tokenUrl="token"'. Never write 'detail=Could not validate' — always 'detail="Could not validate"'. Never write 'schemes=[bcrypt]' — always 'schemes=["bcrypt"]'. Every string literal needs quotes. This is the #1 bug in generated code.
Detect target platform from request context. If Termux/Android, use stdlib or ARM64 pip packages. If Colab/cloud, use any ML packages.
Local scripts that call cloud services (Colab, APIs) should not import heavy ML libraries. They prepare data and trigger remote jobs.
When a project has both local and cloud components, put ML imports in the cloud component only.
Every project MUST have at least one runnable entry point with if __name__ == '__main__' that initializes the system and accepts user input.
The main entry point should wire all modules together — imports, initialization, and a run loop or command dispatch.
Tools that write files or execute commands must actually perform the action, not just describe it. Use subprocess, os.system, or file I/O.
Queue entries use format MODE: filepath - description. The filepath ends at the first ' - ' (space-hyphen-space). Everything after is description. Descriptions can contain hyphens.

When generating PATCH queue entries, put the EXACT filepath first, then space-hyphen-space, then the change description. Example: PATCH: tools/execute_tool.py - use subprocess to actually run shell commands
NEVER hardcode API keys in source files. Use environment variables or .env files. Config files should have empty placeholder values.
Add .env, config.yaml, and any file containing secrets to .gitignore before first commit.
For Gemini API on Termux, use the OpenAI-compatible endpoint: https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai/chat/completions with Bearer token auth. Model: gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview.
